claim: FEM is the industry standard for magnet design, with FEMM, Elmer/FEM, Gmsh+GetFEM++, and FEniCS/Dolfinx as major open-source implementations
MuMax3 and OOMMF are the primary open-source micromagnetic solvers, solving the LLG equation at the nanoscale with GPU (MuMax3) and CPU (OOMMF) acceleration
magpylib is the most comprehensive open-source Python library for analytical magnetostatic field computation, supporting multiple magnet geometries with vectorized performance
Vimag Labs VMSM, ZF SESM, and SpinMag SpinRel represent three approaches to replacing physical magnets: software-controlled excitation, inductive coupling, and reluctance-based design
VESC firmware implements FOC with Clarke/Park transforms, PID current control, and sensorless modes, providing the most mature open-source reference for motor magnetic field control
No single open-source project provides a complete virtual magnet motor system; building blocks exist but require custom integration
Virtual magnet performance claims by Vimag Labs lack independent production-scale validation
Wound rotor designs (VMSM, SESM) introduce resistive I^2*R losses that permanent magnets avoid, representing a fundamental physics limitation
Magnet simulation operates across three scales: nanoscale (micromagnetic/LLG), macroscopic (FEM/Maxwell), and system scale (analytical/closed-form)
Virtual magnet technology represents a paradigm shift from materials science to control theory in motor design
